text: Adopted write-now/index-later: hot path appends raw JSONL; cold path at 02:00 distills and embeds. Two models cannot coexist on CHost, so batching (all writer, then all embed) is mandatory.
CHost has Intel Iris Xe integrated graphics, ~1 GB shared VRAM, no discrete GPU. Qwen2.5-coder:7b loads at 25%/75% CPU/GPU split.

Resolved D11 (Tier 3a backup): Seagate is mounted at D:, sometimes-plugged. Cold-path mirrors .augment/memory/** to D:\Backups\MCProcessor\ via robocopy /MIR. When D: is absent the step logs an info skip; next nightly run with the drive connected catches up automatically. No retention layer (mirror = latest state).
Tier 3a skip-branch self-test entry; safe to ignore.

Validating cold-path Phase 3b: git auto-commit + push of nightly memory artifacts to origin/main. After this run, repo HEAD should advance with a 'cold-path: distill 2026-05-22' commit including all .augment/memory/** changes from today.

Fixed cold-path Phase 3b: Push-Location must target repo root (Split-Split memRoot), not the .augment/ parent. Previous code looked for .augment/.augment/memory and failed. Also added LASTEXITCODE check on git push so commit-then-push-failed is logged distinctly from no-op.

Phase 3b round 2: wrapped git block with EAP=Continue and replaced try/catch driven control flow with LASTEXITCODE checks. Git's stderr warnings (e.g. CRLF/LF) no longer abort the commit. Also restructured to avoid early-return so cold-path always logs run-end.
